messages.content: 修改句式：The biomechanical triboelectric harvesting properties were further demonstrated by capacitor charging and the stable operation of electronics including the commercial LEDs, stopwatch, and calculator, demonstrating its promising application in biomechanical energy harvesting for wearable devices.

The biomechanical triboelectric harvesting properties were further demonstrated through capacitor charging and the successful operation of wearable devices including commercial LEDs, stopwatch, and calculator, thereby showcasing its potential for application in harvesting biomechanical energy for wearable devices.
再润色一下，太像了

The biomechanical triboelectric harvesting properties were further substantiated as it showcased its ability to charge capacitors and effectively operate wearable devices such as commercial LEDs, stopwatch, and calculator. This serves as strong evidence for its promising application in biomechanical energy harvesting for wearable devices.

润色以下语句： Moreover, electrospinning involves the uniaxial stretching of polymer chains, which encourages the generation of the β-phase PVDF-HFP with highest spontaneous polarization and enhances surface charge density.

Additionally, the process of electrospinning includes the uniaxial stretching of polymer chains, which promotes the formation of the β-phase PVDF-HFP with the highest level of spontaneous polarization and augments the surface charge density.

润色以下句式：Firstly, the Cs2InCl5(H2O) nanocrystals and PVDF-HFP particles were dissolved in 5:5 DMF: Acetone with strong stirring at 55 oC for 6 h to prepare the mixture solution (total concentration of 20%, wt./wt.). The homogeneous mixed solution was then collected by an injector with a needle nozzle ready for electrospinning.

To begin with, the Cs2InCl5(H2O) nanocrystals and PVDF-HFP particles were dissolved in a 5:5 DMF: Acetone solution with vigorous stirring at 55°C for a duration of 6 hours. This resulted in the preparation of a mixture solution with a total concentration of 20% (wt./wt.). Subsequently, the well-blended solution was collected using an injector equipped with a needle nozzle, making it ready for the electrospinning process.
润色以下句式：The positive and negative voltages were respectively adjusted to 22 kV and -2 kV, while keeping the distance from the collector to the needle at 15 cm.
The positive and negative voltages were precisely adjusted to 22 kV and -2 kV, respectively, while maintaining a constant distance of 15 cm between the collector and the needle.

润色以下句式：The membrane obtained by electrospinning was exsiccated at 60 oC in the oven for 12 hours. The thickness of prepared CIC@HFP nanofibers was ~ 80 µm and the membrane was cut into 3*3 cm2 for performance evalution.

The electrospun membrane was subjected to a drying process in an oven at 60°C for a duration of 12 hours. This resulted in the formation of CIC@HFP nanofibers with an approximate thickness of 80 µm. Subsequently, the membrane was carefully cut into squares measuring 3*3 cm² for performance evaluation purposes.

润色以下句式：In the electrospinning of CIC@HFP nanofibers, different Cs2InCl5(H2O) concentration (0 wt.%, 0.5 wt.%, 1 wt.%, 1.5 wt.%, 2 wt.%, and 3 wt.%) was modulated and defined as PVDF-HFP, 0.5CIC@HFP, 1CIC@HFP, 1.5CIC@HFP, 2CIC@HFP, 3CIC@HFP.

During the electrospinning process of CIC@HFP nanofibers, various concentrations of Cs2InCl5(H2O) (0 wt.%, 0.5 wt.%, 1 wt.%, 1.5 wt.%, 2 wt.%, and 3 wt.%) were adjusted and referred to as PVDF-HFP, 0.5CIC@HFP, 1CIC@HFP, 1.5CIC@HFP, 2CIC@HFP, and 3CIC@HFP, respectively.

润色以下句式： Powder X-ray diffraction (XRD) was characterized using a D2 Phaser XE-T X-ray diffractometer system to obtain the material phase information, the scan rate and scope of 2θ was respectively kept at 5◦ min-1 and 5 – 80◦.

To obtain material phase information, Powder X-ray Diffraction (XRD) analysis was performed using a D2 Phaser XE-T X-ray diffractometer system. The scan rate was set at 5° min^-1, and the scan range of 2θ was maintained between 5° and 80°.
润色以下句式：The morphology was detected by utilizing scanning electron microscope (SEM, JEOL 7001F) and transmission electron microscope (TEM, Philips CM20, Thermo Scientific FEI Talos F200X).

The morphology analysis was conducted by utilizing both scanning electron microscopy (SEM) with a JEOL 7001F instrument and transmission electron microscopy (TEM) with a Philips CM20 and Thermo Scientific FEI Talos F200X instruments.

润色以下句式：The breaking strength was boosted from the 10.1 MPa of the PVDF-HFP nanofibers to 14.2 MPa of the 1.5CIC@HFP nanofibers. This can be ascribed to the hydrogen bonding effect between the PVDF-HFP chains and the -OH bonds of Cs2InCl5(H2O), resulting in improved mechanical properties.

The breaking strength of the nanofibers was significantly increased from 10.1 MPa in the PVDF-HFP nanofibers to 14.2 MPa in the 1.5CIC@HFP nanofibers. This improvement can be attributed to the hydrogen bonding effect between the PVDF-HFP chains and the -OH bonds of Cs2InCl5(H2O), which enhances the mechanical properties of the nanofibers.

润色以下句式：Cs2InCl5(H2O) blending ratio has a strong effect on the tensile properties of CIC@HFP nanofiber films (Figure 2j). The breaking strength was boosted from the 10.1 MPa of the PVDF-HFP nanofibers to 14.2 MPa of the 1.5CIC@HFP nanofibers. This can be ascribed to the hydrogen bonding effect between the PVDF-HFP chains and the -OH bonds of Cs2InCl5(H2O), resulting in improved mechanical properties.

The blending ratio of Cs2InCl5(H2O) has a significant influence on the tensile properties of CIC@HFP nanofiber films, as depicted in Figure 2j. It is observed that the breaking strength of the nanofibers is enhanced from 10.1 MPa in the PVDF-HFP nanofibers to 14.2 MPa in the 1.5CIC@HFP nanofibers. This improvement can be attributed to the formation of hydrogen bonds between the PVDF-HFP chains and the -OH bonds of Cs2InCl5(H2O), consequently resulting in improved mechanical properties.

润色以下句式：The water vapor transfer rate (WVTR) and air permeability (AP) were characterized for the breathability of the fabricated nanofibers (Figure 2k). The WVTR and AP performance should be ascribed to the porous fibrous network architectures. The typical WVTR and AP of the 1.5CIC@HFP nanofibers at 25 oC, 50% relative humidity (RH) can attain over 18.0 kg m-2 d-1 and ~ 4.3 mL s-1.

The breathability of the fabricated nanofibers was assessed by characterizing the water vapor transfer rate (WVTR) and air permeability (AP), as shown in Figure 2k. The excellent WVTR and AP performance can be attributed to the porous fibrous network architecture. Specifically, the 1.5CIC@HFP nanofibers exhibit remarkable WVTR and AP values at 25°C and 50% relative humidity (RH), with an ability to achieve over 18.0 kg m^-2 d^-1 and approximately 4.3 mL s^-1, respectively.

润色以下句式：two-electrode mode TENG comprises of CIC@HFP nanofibers and nylon 6,6 (or other electropositive materials) as triboelectric layers, respectively. When CIC@HFP is in contact with nylon 6,6 nanofibers, charges are transferred from the nylon 6,6 nanofibers to CIC@HFP nanofibers.

In the two-electrode mode TENG, the configuration consists of CIC@HFP nanofibers as one of the triboelectric layers and nylon 6,6 (or other electropositive materials) as the other layer. When the CIC@HFP nanofibers come into contact with the nylon 6,6 nanofibers, the transfer of charges occurs from the nylon 6,6 nanofibers to the CIC@HFP nanofibers.
润色以下句式：In addition, the increased fiber diameter of the CIC@HFP NFs will reduce the contact area in the contact-separation processes.
Furthermore, the larger diameter of the CIC@HFP nanofibers results in a reduced contact area during the contact-separation processes.

The calculation of P is performed using the following Equation (2):
